channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Xi hails China’s ‘unyielding’ ties with Russia as Putin visits Beijing
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping on Wednesday welcomed Russian President Vladimir Putin in Beijing at the start of a visit focused on energy security. The meeting comes on the heels of US president Donald Trump’s visit to the Chinese capital. France 24’s Andy Hilliar takes a look at the latest developments.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'Crony capitalism and oligarchy': 'Remarkable correlation' between Adani's rise & Modi’s ascendancy
item.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Paranjoy Guha-Thakurta, Indian journalist, investigative author and filmmaker. He offers a sweeping critique of the nexus between oligarchic wealth, political power, and weakened institutional accountability in contemporary India. Drawing on the allegations surrounding billionaire industrialist Gautam Adani, U.S. sanctions investigations, and the politics of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, he argues that “crony capitalism and oligarchy” have become deeply embedded within the fabric of the Indian state.
item.dc:creator: François PICARD

item.title: Race to contain ebola outbreak in war-torn DRC where weak infrastructure hampers response
item.description: Annette Young welcomes Julie Drouet, DRC Director for Action Against Hunger. She offers a sobering assessment of the early Ebola outbreak response in the Democratic Republic of Congo. Speaking from the frontline of a rapidly evolving health emergency, Drouet describes a humanitarian system racing against time in one of the world’s most fragile and conflict-affected regions. They are not only facing epidemiological challenges of containing Ebola, but also the deeper structural vulnerabilities that make outbreaks in eastern DRC uniquely perilous.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iran ‘prefers diplomacy’ but if there’s a war, Tehran says it’s ready
item.description: Iran and the US traded threats as President Donald Trump warned of renewed strikes a day after he said he held off a major assault in hopes of a peace deal. Reporting from Tehran, FRANCE 24’s Reza Sayah says Iranian officials have maintained their position that “they prefer diplomacy. They don't see a military solution to this conflict,” he explains. “But if there's a war, they're ready for it.”
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Xi welcomes Putin in Beijing, hails China’s ‘unyielding’ ties with Russia
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping on Wednesday welcomed Russian President Vladimir Putin in Beijing at the start of a visit focused on energy security. While Xi hailed his country's "unyielding" ties with Russia, Putin praised the "unprecedentedly high level" of bilateral cooperation despite "unfavourable external factors", without naming any third country.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Arsenal win Premier League title for first time since 2004
item.description: Thanks to Manchester City's 1-1 draw at Bournemouth, Arsenal have won their 14th Premier League title.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: South Korean labour minister to mediate talks as Samsung faces strike threat
item.description: South ​Korea's ​labour minister will personally mediate talks ​between ‌Samsung ⁠Electronics and ‌its South Korean ⁠union, the ministry announced Thursday after the union threatened a strike following the collapse of talks on bonus payouts.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Taiwan leader says ‘foreign forces’ cannot decide island’s future amid China pressure
item.description: Taiwanese President Lai Ching-te said on Wednesday that “foreign forces” could not decide the future of the democratic island, days after US President Donald Trump suggested arms sales to Taiwan could be used as leverage in talks with China. Beijing claims Taiwan as its territory and has threatened to seize it by force.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Middle East war live: Chinese, South Korean oil tankers pass through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Three oil tankers – two Chinese and one South Korean – crossed the Strait of Hormuz Wednesday, according to maritime tracking data, as Iran threatened to open “new fronts” if the US resumed strikes. President Donald Trump is set to attend the G7 summit in France next month, despite tensions between Washington and its allies on the Iran war. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Pentagon says it reduced US troop brigades in Europe from four to three
item.description: The Pentagon said on Tuesday it was cutting the number of US Brigade Combat Teams stationed in Europe from four to three, returning troop levels to those seen in 2021 as Washington presses European allies to assume greater responsibility for regional defence. The move will temporarily delay the deployment of additional US forces to Poland.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: EU reaches deal on US trade pact as Trump threatens new tariffs by July 4
item.description: EU lawmakers and member states reached a deal early on Wednesday to implement the bloc’s trade pact with the United States, ending months of wrangling over the accord as President Donald Trump threatened fresh tariffs unless it was approved by July 4. The compromise followed late-night negotiations between EU capitals and parliament negotiators.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Trump tightens grip on Republican Party after Thomas Massie primary defeat
item.description: US President Donald Trump tightened his grip on the Republican Party on Tuesday after Kentucky voters ousted Congressman Thomas Massie, one of the few conservatives willing to openly challenge him. The defeat, projected by NBC and CNN, followed setbacks for other Republican dissenters, including Indiana lawmakers and Louisiana Senator Bill Cassidy.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: New York woman dies after falling into open manhole after exiting car
item.description: A 56-year-old woman died after falling into an open maintenance hole on a busy Midtown Manhattan street, New York police and utility officials said on Tuesday, as authorities investigated how the incident occurred. The woman fell into the hole after parking her SUV near Fifth Avenue and East 52nd Street late Monday and was pronounced dead after firefighters pulled her out.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: IRS will not pursue Trump for back taxes under settlement agreement
item.description: The US Internal Revenue Service will not pursue President Donald Trump, his family or the Trump Organisation for back taxes under an agreement announced by the Justice Department on Tuesday. Trump, his sons Eric and Donald Jr., and the Trump Organisation sued the IRS in January over leaked tax returns, seeking $10 billion in damages.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Israel far-right finance minister says ICC prosecutor seeks arrest warrant against him
item.description: Israel's far-right Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich said on Tuesday the International Criminal Court prosecutor had sought an arrest warrant against him, blaming the Palestinian Authority for the move. Smotrich said Israel would respond by ordering the evacuation of the Palestinian Bedouin community of Khan al-Ahmar in the occupied West Bank.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: 'Huge mismatch' between Iran, US and Gulf States' expectations, analyst says
item.description: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney, H. A. Hellyer, Senior Associate Fellow at the Royal United Services Institute for Defence and Security Studies in London, says that "there is a very huge mismatch not just between the expectations of the Iranians and the Americans, but also among Gulf Arab states which will have to live with whatever mess is left after this".
item.dc:creator: Sharon GAFFNEY

item.title: Multilateralism at work: Eurogroup chief on G7 meeting, Europe's priorities and challenges
item.description: G7 finance ministers and central bankers have wrapped up their two-day meetings in Paris as the war in Iran and the energy crisis continue to weigh on the global economy. Eurogroup President Kyriakos Pierrakakis, who represented the 21-member euro area in the talks, told FRANCE 24's François Picard and Yuka Royer that despite some differences, there was a shared understanding that a multilateral approach was key to facing the ongoing challenges.
item.dc:creator: Yuka ROYER

item.title: Arsenal win Premier League title after Manchester City draw at Bournemouth
item.description: Arsenal won the Premier League for the first time since 2004 on Tuesday after Manchester City drew with Bournemouth. The team has finished second for the past three seasons.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: DR Congo's latest Ebola outbreak tough to tackle
item.description: In tonight's edition: The World Health Organization is warning that it's unlikely the Ebola outbreak will be over in the next two months. Also, a Kenyan transport strike triggered by rising fuel prices due to the Middle East war is paused after causing four deaths and major economic disruption. Plus pre-trial hearings open in The Hague over a former Libyan prison commander who prosecutors say was known as the "angel of death" by terrified detainees.
item.dc:creator: Georja CALVIN-SMITH

item.title: Arsenal win Premier League after Manchester City draw at Bournemouth
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Joint Nigeria-US strikes kill 175 Islamic State group fighters, both militaries say
item.description: Some 175 militants belonging to the Islamic State (IS) group were killed in joint Nigerian-US strikes in Nigeria's northeast in recent days, both militaries said Tuesday. Washington sent troops to the West African nation in February as armed groups stepped up their attacks on communities across the country.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: UK far-right rally: AI images, old footage exaggerate 'Unite the Kingdom' turnout
item.description: Waving Union Jacks, tens of thousands of supporters of far-right activist Tommy Robinson descended on London's streets last Saturday for a rally known as "Unite the Kingdom". Addressing the crowd, Robinson said "we are here in our millions", and his supporters followed suit online, sharing images of enormous rallies. In reality, London's Metropolitan Police believe 60,000 demonstrators attended, and several of the viral images online are either AI-generated or using old footage.
item.dc:creator: Vedika BAHL
